silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from the bronze qualys_vulnerabilities ingest and filter to records with a valid vulnerability identifier and an ingestion timestamp
Normalize severity labels into canonical tiers (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) and deduplicate so each vulnerability instance per asset per day is one row
Truncate ingestion timestamp to day to produce snapshot_date as the daily grain
Produce daily counts and status flags (OPEN, FIXED) per vulnerability per asset to enable time series trends

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_counts_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Aggregate from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily into weekly buckets and calculate weekly deltas by severity

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily as the canonical daily snapshot of active vulnerabilities per asset
Filter to records where vulnerability state is OPEN/ACTIVE
Aggregate to daily total counts across all assets to produce a time series of total open vulnerabilities
Annotate each daily row with a rolling 7-day and 30-day windowed total to support trend analysis

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_total_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly aggregation of total open vulnerabilities from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains one canonical row per vulnerability per asset per day
Group by asset identifier and snapshot_date to compute daily vulnerability counts per asset
Flag asset criticality where available (e.g., production vs non-production) and add as a dimension for filtering
Produce derived column 'vulnerability_density' as vulnerabilities per asset normalized by asset role for benchmarking

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_by_asset_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly rollup of per-asset vulnerability counts and density for dashboarding and SLAs

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily and filter to severity = CRITICAL and state = OPEN
For each snapshot_date, count unique vulnerability instances affecting assets
Restrict the time window to the last 30 days for the metric timeframe
Tag each vulnerability with first_seen_date and compute age in days to enable aging trend lines

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_open_critical_vulns_30d_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly summary of open critical vulnerabilities over rolling 30-day windows

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: From silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily, identify vulnerability instances with both first_seen_date and fixed_date populated
Calculate remediation_time_days as the difference between fixed_date and first_seen_date for each vulnerability instance
Aggregate remediation_time_days to daily median and mean values grouped by severity to produce time series
Exclude anomalies such as remediation_time_days > 365 or missing dates from primary calculations and surface separately

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_mttr_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly MTTR (median/mean) per severity derived from the daily silver table

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Ingest raw snyk_vulnerabilities and normalize vulnerability identifiers and severity tiers to canonical severity labels
Deduplicate vulnerability occurrences per project/component per day to one canonical row
Create snapshot_date truncated to day and compute daily counts per severity and project
Enrich with dependency/package metadata where present to support triage grouping

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_snyk_vulnerability_trends_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly rollups of Snyk vulnerabilities by severity and project

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily which includes one row per vulnerable package/component per day
Identify dependencies linked to production applications and tag exposure type (direct vs transitive)
Aggregate daily counts of vulnerable dependencies by project and severity to form a time series
Compute proportion of high/critical vulnerable dependencies to total dependencies per project as a benchmark signal

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_dependency_exposure_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly exposure summaries by project and severity derived from silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: From silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily, identify vulnerabilities that are classified as PATCHABLE and have a patch_available flag

For each snapshot_date and asset, compute patched_count and applicable_count where patched_count is number of PATCHABLE vulnerabilities marked fixed and applicable_count is total PATCHABLE vulnerabilities
Calculate daily patch_compliance_rate as patched_count / applicable_count and annotate by asset group and OS
Create 30-day rolling averages of patch_compliance_rate to smooth the trend

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_patch_compliance_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly patch compliance rates by asset group derived from daily silver snapshots

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: From bronze qualys_vulnerabilities, determine latest_scan_date per asset and ingest into the silver table as last_scan_date
Flag assets where last_scan_date is older than desired SLA windows (e.g., 90 days) and mark as 'stale_scan'
Aggregate daily counts of stale_scan assets to produce a time series for monitoring compliance with scan frequency
Provide asset-level detail including asset owner and environment for triage

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_scan_coverage_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly summaries of scan coverage and stale-scan asset counts

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_cve_exposure_daily
calculation_steps: Create silver_cve_exposure_daily by unioning normalized rows from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily and silver_snyk_vulnerabilities_daily using canonical CVE identifiers
For each CVE instance compute age_in_days since first_seen_date and categorize into buckets (0-30, 31-90, 91-365, >365)
Aggregate counts per bucket per snapshot_date and annotate by severity and asset criticality to enable exposure heatmaps
Include source_system column to differentiate exposure coming from infrastructure scans vs developer dependency scans

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_cve_exposure_age_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly exposure age distribution across CVEs aggregated from combined silver source
